dry_bulk.confluence_note: While quantitative indices show an aggressive expansion regime with BDI at the 99.7th percentile, qualitative fundamentals remain consistently negative due to high Chinese portside inventories.
dry_bulk.summary: Capesize and Panamax rates have hit near-record levels with ROC60 values of +66.5% and +22.4% respectively. However, weak Chinese steel production (-3.6% YOY) and swelling iron ore inventories (+20.3% YOY) create a significant disconnect between spot pricing and underlying commodity demand.
dry_bulk.outlook: The market is likely to face a correction as spot momentum exhausts and fundamental oversupply of iron ore in China weighs on fresh fixtures.
dry_bulk.watch: China steel production figures and iron ore inventory drawdown at major ports.

tanker.confluence_note: Extreme quantitative expansion (Clean Tanker Z-score +3.29) is strongly supported by positive fundamentals stemming from geopolitical disruptions and unnavigable straits.
tanker.summary: The tanker market has entered a super-cycle, with Clean Tanker rates soaring 150.1% over 60 days. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East have disrupted traditional routes, significantly increasing ton-mile demand as the market adjusts to alternative, longer-haul trade lanes. Segment breadth remains important: clean tankers at 2163.0 (ROC60 +150.1%), alongside dirty tankers at 2907.0 (ROC60 +79.6%).
tanker.outlook: Bullish sentiment remains dominant as structural supply tightness and geopolitical risk premiums continue to drive rates to 5-year highs.
tanker.watch: Middle East geopolitical developments and US/China crude oil export/import volumes.

macro_note: A stark bifurcation exists between tankers, driven by supply-side geopolitical shocks, and dry bulk, which is riding spot momentum despite softening Chinese industrial macro indicators. High oil prices and Middle East volatility remain the primary systemic risks to global trade flows.
